description_long: This object is a weathered, upright stone monument resembling a traditional gravestone or memorial obelisk, with a rounded top and a rectangular shaft. The front face features an arched recessed panel and an inscription, partially legible, with the name 'A.J. Sloan' and additional text below. The stone surface shows signs of aging, discoloration, and erosion, particularly on the back and base. The base is a thick, roughly rectangular slab, slightly wider than the shaft, with irregular edges and a rough texture. The overall color is a mix of gray and off-white, with darker staining and patina in recessed areas. The monument appears solid and heavy, likely carved from a single block of stone, and is designed to be a permanent fixture in an outdoor setting such as a cemetery or park.
description: A weathered stone memorial obelisk with a rounded top, inscribed front, and thick rectangular base, showing signs of age and discoloration.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched inscription panel, engraved text, and the most detailed surface features.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the shaft and the thickness of the base slab.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain, unadorned surface with visible discoloration and no inscriptions.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the shaft and the irregular base outline.
attribute_inscription: Front face has an engraved inscription with the name 'A.J. Sloan' and additional text below, in uppercase serif letters. The text is partially eroded and only partly legible.
attribute_ornamentation: None visible beyond the arched recessed panel framing the inscription.
attribute_base_design: The base is a thick, roughly rectangular stone slab with irregular, chipped edges and a rough, weathered surface. It is slightly wider than the shaft and provides stable support.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is smoothly rounded, forming a semicircular arch with no additional decoration or features.

license_info.creator_display_name: Santa Cruz Museum of Art and History
